mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 50% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of triggered instances over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and falling consumer price inflation indicates robust demand growth without monetary tightening pressure, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Interaction term loses statistical significance with p-value above 0.10 in out-of-sample regression or direction accuracy falls below 45 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in the index of industrial production reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and corporate output, which drive positive S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days during economic recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of observations during recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 195.0, market participants anticipate aggressive Federal Reserve tightening to combat inflation, causing immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of instances where CPIAUCSL crosses the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates strengthening economic activity and rising corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60 trading day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over consecutive 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in housing starts and quarterly GDP growth signals a broad-based demand contraction that reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint decline produces positive forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in industrial production exceeding 1.0 percent signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output that immediately triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of triggered instances over a 3-year sample.
